model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization-side changes already gave a strong gain, so the next clean step is a modest capacity increase. Moving to three pooling blocks with a slightly wider stem and a slightly larger head should improve feature quality within the same short-horizon training setup while keeping the architecture simple and still compact.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The current configuration is already using a short-horizon high-throughput regime successfully. A minimal next step is to keep the same learning dynamics but switch to AdamW and slightly stronger decay, which often improves generalization for batch-normalized CNNs without forcing a broader retune.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The previous batch-size increase was the highest-yield change so far, and there is still room to exploit the fixed-step budget by showing the model more examples. A moderate step to batch size 384, paired with a small learning-rate increase, is a simple continuation of the same successful direction without a major architectural rewrite.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The fixed-step throughput changes are now strong, so the next low-complexity move is to give the model a bit more representational capacity. A modest width increase and a slightly larger hidden layer should let the network use the extra data exposure better without changing the training loop or optimizer behavior.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is now substantially stronger and trained on many more examples within the fixed step budget, so a small amount of output-side regularization is a clean next move. Mild label smoothing often improves CIFAR-10 validation loss for AdamW-trained CNNs without requiring an optimizer or architecture retune.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Label smoothing moved validation loss in the wrong direction, so the clean next step is to remove it and return to the stronger baseline configuration before testing further ideas. This preserves the current gains and avoids carrying extra regularization that does not pay off in this short-budget regime.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The warmup-plus-cosine schedule substantially improved the short-horizon optimization, which makes a modest learning-rate increase the cleanest next probe. Raising the peak AdamW rate slightly should test whether the current schedule was under-driving the larger-batch model without adding structural complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Validation loss kept improving as the peak learning rate increased under the warmed-up cosine schedule, so the next high-value probe is another small step upward. This tests whether the current regime is still optimization-limited without changing the model or adding complexity elsewhere.","secondary_modes":["summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-3","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The optimization schedule is now substantially stronger, so a small increase in decoupled weight decay is a clean next test for better generalization. This keeps the learned optimization recipe intact while probing whether the current larger model and large-batch regime are slightly under-regularized.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Stronger weight decay degraded validation loss, so the next step is to restore the better-performing regularization setting before trying anything else. This returns to the strongest visible configuration and avoids carrying a known regression forward.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current optimization recipe is strong and stable, so a cheap capacity increase in the classifier head is a reasonable next probe. Increasing only the hidden layer size adds little runtime compared with widening all convolutional stages, while still testing whether the model is now bottlenecked in the final representation-to-logit mapping.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The larger classifier head was a clear regression, so the next step is to restore the previously best-performing capacity setting. This keeps the stronger optimizer and schedule while removing a head-size increase that did not pay off.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 448","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest gains in this run came from using the fixed 256-step budget to expose the model to more training examples. With the optimizer and schedule now substantially improved, a moderate batch-size increase is the cleanest remaining probe of that same high-yield direction without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The model improved again after increasing batch size, so the next focused test is to raise the peak learning rate to match the larger effective batch. This stays within the already successful warmup-plus-cosine regime and probes whether the new throughput setting is slightly under-tuned on optimization scale.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Both the larger batch and the last learning-rate increase improved validation loss, so the most direct remaining probe is another modest step up in peak rate. This stays on the strongest trajectory found so far and tests whether the current regime still benefits from more aggressive optimization scale.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Validation loss has continued to improve along the larger-batch, higher-learning-rate trajectory, so the most efficient penultimate probe is one more modest increase in peak rate. This follows the dominant successful trend without disturbing the architecture or schedule structure.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The strongest and most consistent improvement trajectory in the later run has been repeated modest peak learning-rate increases under the same warmup-plus-cosine schedule. With one step left, the highest-value final probe is to continue that trend once more rather than introducing a qualitatively different change.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}
